Title: On Blind Frame Synchronization of LDPC Codes
Authors: Rodrigue Imad, Sebastien Houcke
Abstract: We consider in this letter the problem of blind frame synchronization of Low Density Parity-Check (LDPC) codes. We present and compare new methods of blind frame synchronization based on the calculation of Likelihood functions of the syndrome. Simulation results show that the four proposed synchronization techniques are effective once applied to LDPC codes. The method that involves the maximizatio...

Title: Protograph LDPC-Coded BICM-ID With Irregular CSK Mapping in Visible Light Communication Systems
Authors: Lin Dai, Yi Fang, Zhaojie Yang, Pingping Chen, Yonghui Li
Abstract: This paper designs and analyzes the bit-interleaved coded modulation with iterative demapping and decoding (BICM-ID) framework under color-shift-keying (CSK) modulation to achieve spectrally efficient transmissions in visible light communication (VLC) systems. Specifically, we investigate the protograph low-density parity-check (LDPC) codes, bit-to-symbol mapping schemes, and constellation labelin...

Title: Parity Check Aided SC-Flip Decoding Algorithms for Polar Codes
Authors: Bin Dai, Chenyu Gao, Zhiyuan Yan, Rongke Liu
Abstract: When polar codes are decoded by the successive cancellation (SC) decoding algorithm, erroneously decoded non-frozen bits are caused by either channel noise or error propagation. SC-Flip algorithms aim to improve error performance by first identifying erroneous hard decisions due to channel noise and then flipping them during the decoding process to reduce error propagation. In existing SC-Flip alg...

Title: Balanced Product Quantum Codes
Authors: Nikolas P. Breuckmann, Jens N. Eberhardt
Abstract: This work provides the first explicit and non-random family of [[N,K,D]] LDPC quantum codes which encode K ∈ Θ(N4/5) logical qubits with distance D ∈ Ω(N3/5). The family is constructed by amalgamating classical codes and Ramanujan graphs via an operation called balanced product. Title: Sign-Preserving Min-Sum Decoders
Authors: Franklin Cochachin, Emmanuel Boutillon, David Declercq
Abstract: This paper proposes a new finite precision iterative decoder for low-density parity-check (LDPC) codes. The proposed decoder, named Sign-Preserving Min-Sum (SP-MS), significantly improves the decoding performance compared to the classical Offset Min-Sum (OMS) decoder when messages are quantized on  $q=2$ , 3, or 4 bits. The particularity of the SP-MS decoder is that messages cannot take the 0 valu...

Title: Primitive Rateless Codes
Authors: Mahyar Shirvanimoghaddam
Abstract: In this paper, we propose primitive rateless (PR) codes. A PR code is characterized by the message length and a primitive polynomial over  $\mathbf {GF}(2)$ , which can generate a potentially limitless number of coded symbols. We show that codewords of a PR code truncated at any arbitrary length can be represented as subsequences of a maximum-length sequence ( $m$ -sequence). We characterize the H...

Title: Partially Information Coupled Bit-Interleaved Polar Coded Modulation
Authors: Xiaowei Wu, Min Qiu, Jinhong Yuan
Abstract: In this paper, we propose partially information coupled bit-interleaved polar coded modulation (PIC-BIPCM), which is a class of spatially coupled polar coding schemes designed for  $2^{Q}$ -ary modulations. Specifically, we consider PIC-BIPCM schemes respectively constructed with three BIPCM schemes: direct BIPCM, punctured BIPCM, and multi-kernel BIPCM. We analyze the error performance of the pro...

Title: HD-Code: End-to-End High Density Code for DNA Storage
Authors: Jianjun Wu, Shufang Zhang, Tao Zhang, Yuhong Liu
Abstract: With the rapid development of digital information techniques, the use of DNA media for information storage is considered as the future direction of data storage. Existing DNA storage schemes simply map compressed binary multimedia data into DNA base data, which has the disadvantages of data loss, low logical storage density and high cost of synthesis. This paper presents an end-to-end high density...

Title: Sequential Decoding of Short Length Binary Codes: Performance Versus Complexity
Authors: Bo Lian, Frank R. Kschischang
Abstract: Sequential decoding of short length binary codes for the additive white Gaussian noise channel is considered. A variant of the variable-bias term (VBT) metric is introduced, producing useful trade-offs between performance and computational complexity. Comparisons are made with tail-biting convolutional codes decoded with a wrap-around Viterbi algorithm (WAVA) and with polar codes under successive-...

Title: Systematic Polar Coded Modulation for Informed Receivers
Authors: Shin-Lin Shieh, Yu-Chih Huang, Po-Ning Chen, Yu-Ming Li
Abstract: The problem of coding for informed receivers (IR) is considered, where a transmitter multicasts a bunch of messages to its associated receivers, each of which already has a subset of messages as side information. Title: Sparse Kronecker-Product Coding for Unsourced Multiple Access
Authors: Zeyu Han, Xiaojun Yuan, Chongbin Xu, Shuchao Jiang, Xin Wang
Abstract: In this letter, a sparse Kronecker-product (SKP) coding scheme is proposed for unsourced multiple access. Specifically, the data of each active user is encoded as the Kronecker product of two component codewords with one being sparse and the other being forward-error-correction (FEC) coded. Title: A New Two-User Binary Polar Coded Multiple Access Scheme With Power and Rate Allocation
Authors: Benjamin K. Ng, Chan-Tong Lam
Abstract: A new polar-code-based scheme with power and rate allocation is proposed to enhance the block error rate (BLER) performance in a two-user binary Gaussian multiple access channel (GMAC). Title: Decoding Polar Codes for a Generalized Gilbert-Elliott Channel With Unknown Parameter
Authors: Yong Fang, Jun Chen
Abstract: Decoding of polar codes, a class of capacity-achieving channel codes, typically requires the perfect knowledge of channel parameter in advance. This paper aims to investigate how to decode polar codes when channel parameter is unknown. Title: Deep Learning Analysis for Split-Manufactured Layouts With Routing Perturbation
Authors: Haocheng Li, Satwik Patnaik, Mohammed Ashraf, Haoyu Yang, Johann Knechtel, Bei Yu, Ozgur Sinanoglu, Evangeline F. Y. Young

Title: Review of Joint Encoding and Encryption for Image Transmission using Chaotic Map, LDPC and AES Encryption
Authors: Kanchan Yadav, Trushita Chaware
Abstract: In today’s era of communication, sharing of information is unavoidable. Analysis of security and reliability while communicating is essential. Information may get altered, copied and copyright can be infringed. Title: An Energy-saving Approach for Error control Codes in Wireless Sensor Networks
Authors: Satwinder Kaur, Deepak B Kuttan, Nitin Mittal
Abstract: Wireless Sensor Networks (WSNs) have limited energy resource which requires authentic data transmission at a minimum cost. Title: Fault Organized Decoders Using Analog VLSI Implementations- A Survey
Authors: A. Deepika, K. Manjunathachari
Abstract: VLSI technology had a key role in the design of most of today’s high-tech electronic circuits. Authors: Ural Mutlu, Yasin Kabalcı
Abstract: The main design criteria of the Fifth-generation (5G) New Radio (NR) specifications are to improve the requirements that enable high-speed, high-reliability, and low-latency communications. Title: Evaluating the Productivity of HDL Efficient Coding Models for 5G Information Networks
Authors: Ilya Pyatin, Juliy Boiko, Oleksander Eromenko
Abstract: The article studies the principles of constructing encoders and decoders using the hardware description language (HDL). Models of efficient coding systems for information networks of 5G technology are proposed. Title: Iterative Soft Decoding of Single Parity Check Convolutional Concatenated Code
Authors: Junmei Chen, Hao Chen, Zongpeng Li
Abstract: By establishing a single parity check relationship between convolutional codewords, a concatenated code, termed single parity check convolutional code (SPC-CC), is proposed. By jointly en/decoding the SPC and CC, as well as carefully allocating redundant information between the pair, we can improve BER more promptly during iterative decoding. Title: Effectiveness of Using Codes With a Sparse Check Matrix for Protection against Algebraic Manipulations
Authors: Alla Levina, Gleb Ryaskin, Sergey Taranov, Anna Polubaryeva
Abstract: This article presents codes with a sparse check matrix and demonstrates its possibilities for analysis error masking probability in the case of algebraic manipulations. The illustrated scheme is based on bent functions calculating redundancy using boolean functions. The paper presents the resistance of created code construction to various types of errors. Title: Parallel Concatenated Code Combining Polar Code and LDPC Code on AWGN Channel
Authors: Luyao Ma, Lijun Zhang
Abstract: Both polar code and Low-Density Parity-Check (LDPC) code have been adopted in 3GPP eMBB scenario. Some scholars have proposed a serial concatenation structure combining these two codes. However, the inner code needs to add redundant bits to protect the redundant bits of the outer code in the serial concatenation. Authors: Si-Yu Zhang, Behnam Shahrrava
Abstract: Intuitively, for polar decoding, an accurate signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) estimation is necessary when using belief propagation (BP) polar decoders. It has been claimed in literature that the BP decoding is sensitive to SNR underestimation. Authors: Hai Tian, Danfeng Zhao, Haoxiang Jia

Authors: Wallaa Y. Alebady, Ahmed A. Hamed
Abstract: Although the polar codes can achieve the Binary-Input Discrete Memoryless Channel (BI-DMLC) capacity, their performance deteriorates at the finite-code lengths, especially in small SNRs. Title: About Usage of Metrics in Decoding of LDPC Codes in Two-State Channels with Memory
Authors: Alina M. Veresova, Anna A. Fominykh, Andrei A. Ovchinnikov
Abstract: The applying of low-density parity-check codes is considered over two-state channels with memory described by Markov and Gilbert models. The error probability using different strategies of syndrome decoding is estimated. The method of choosing the coset leaders is proposed which provides the error probability close to maximum-likelihood decoding for the considered codes.

Authors: Emna Ben Yacoub, Gianluigi Liva
Abstract: Binary and a ternary message passing decoding algorithms for generalized low-density parity-check codes are defined and analyzed. In all algorithms, the variable nodes exploit soft channel information. However, the exchanged messages between variable and check nodes are binary or ternary. Title: Deep Neural Network Based Decoding of Short 5G LDPC Codes
Authors: Kirill Andreev, Alexey Frolov, German Svistunov, Kedi Wu, Jing Liang
Abstract: We investigate the application of machine learning techniques (in particular, deep neural networks, DNN) to improve the decoding algorithms of short quasi-cyclic low-density paritycheck (LDPC) codes adopted to the 5G standard. Title: New Code-Based Cryptosystem Based on Binary Image of Generalized Reed-Solomon Code
Authors: Fedor Ivanov, Eugenii Krouk, Victor Zyablov
Abstract: McEliece cryptosystem represents a smart open key system based on the hardness of the decoding of an arbitrary linear code, which is believed to be able to resist the advent of quantum computers. Title: Novel order statistics–based detector and coded modulation for a DHA FH OFDMA system
Authors: Dmitry Osipov
Abstract: Coded DHA FH OFDMA communication systems that use order statistics-based detectors are promising candidates for applications such as IoT and M2M. Authors: Gleb Balitskiy, Alexey Frolov, Pavel Rybin
Abstract: In modern coding theory, linear code constructions are mainly used. Their properties and fundamental theoretical limits are well studied, and they are successfully applied to different classes of problems. Title: SDN/NFV based Secure SCMA design in SDR
Authors: Müge Erel-Özçevik, Ferdi Tekçe
Abstract: Due to increased mMTC devices in 5G, OFDMA has not been a solution for resource allocation in SDR. Authors: Szu-Lin Su, Nan-Hsiung Huang, Jing-Fen Yang
Abstract: Power-line communication (PLC) system can exchange information over the existing electrical grid without much extra implementation cost. Authors: Marcus de Ree, Georgios Mantas, Jonathan Rodriguez, Saud Althunibat, Marwa K. Qaraqe, Abdullah Alhasanat, Saif M. Al-Kuwari, Moath Alsafasfeh, Gabriele Oligeri, Seda Tusha, Muhammad Usman, Fatima Abu Taha, Samiha Alfalahat, Tasneem Alshamaseen, Malak Qaisi

Authors: Wenshan Jiang, Tao Xu, Qun Liu, Xue Zhao, Jing Zhang, Qi Yang, Xingwen Yi, Kun Qiu
Abstract: We propose an end-to-end autoencoder for optical OFDM communication system, which is trained based on bit-wise mutual information (BMI). The simulation results show that the autoencoder can optimize the constellations and achieve BER performance improvement.

Authors: Ruotong Wang, Qinghua Tian, Zexuan Jing, Dong Guo, Feng Tian, Leijing Yang, Xiangju Xin
Abstract: In roder to adjust the log-likelihood ratios (LLRs), we design a hierarchical min-sum algorithm based on dynamic scaling factor (DSF-HMS). The results show that the proposed algorithm can improve the decoding performance.

Authors: Lingguo Cao, Hengying Xu, Chenglin Bai, Lishan Yang, Weibin Sun, Xinkuo Yu, Peng Qin, Xue Tang

Authors: Wei Zhang, Pingping Chen, Zhaopeng Xie, Feng Chen
Abstract: In this paper, we design protograph low-density parity-check (LDPC) codes for two-user downlink nonorthogonal multiple access (NOMA). Authors: Hai He, Ming Jiang, Mingyang Zhu, Chunming Zhao

Authors: Chunfang Fu, Chen Gong, Nuo Huang, Jianghua Luo, Zhengyuan Xu

Authors: Nam-Il KIM, Jin-Up KIM
Abstract: According to its excellent error recovery capability, Low Density Parity Check code has been adopted as a channel coding method for 5G NR data channels. This paper proposes a novel early termination scheme of the layered quasi-cyclic LDPC code decoder for a 5G NR data channel. Abstract: Joint signature design and activity detection with deep learning has been extensively investigated for grant-free Non-Orthogonal Multiple Access (NOMA) in massive machine-type communications. Authors: Naifu Zhang, Meixia Tao
Abstract: A major bottleneck in distributed learning is the communication overhead of exchanging intermediate model update parameters between the worker nodes and the parameter server. Title: Analysis of Delayed Bit-Interleaved Coded Modulation for APSK

Abstract: In this work, we study delayed bit-interleaved coded modulation (DBICM) on amplitude and phase-shift keying (APSK) constellation. Title: On the Universality of Spatially Coupled LDPC Codes Over Intersymbol Interference Channels

Abstract: In this paper, we derive the exact input/output transfer functions of the optimal a-posteriori probability channel detector for a general ISI channel with erasures. Considering three channel impulse responses of different memory as an example, we compute the BP and MAP thresholds for regular spatially coupled LDPC codes with joint iterative detection and decoding. Authors: Hang Zhang, Afshin Abdi, Faramarz Fekri
Abstract: This paper proposes a general framework to design a sparse sensing matrix ${\mathbf {A}} \in \mathbb{R}^{m\times n}$, in a linear measurement system ${\mathbf {y = Ax}}^{\sharp } + {\mathbf {w}}$, where ${\mathbf {y}} \in \mathbb{R}^{n}, {\mathbf {x}}^{\sharp } \in \mathbb{R}^{n}$, and w denote the measurements, the signal with certain structures, and the measurement noise, respectively. Title: Irregularly Clipped Sparse Regression Codes
Authors: Wencong Li, Lei Liu, Brian M. Kurkoski
Abstract: Recently, it was found that clipping can significantly improve the section error rate (SER) performance of sparse regression (SR) codes if an optimal clipping threshold is chosen. In this paper, we propose irregularly clipped SR codes, where multiple clipping thresholds are applied to symbols according to a distribution, to further improve the SER performance of SR codes. Title: New Dual Relationships for Error-Correcting Wiretap Codes
Authors: Morteza Shoushtari, Willie K. Harrison
Abstract: In this paper, we consider the equivocation of finite blocklength coset codes when used over binary erasure wiretap channels. We make use of the equivocation matrix in comparing codes that are suitable for scenarios with noisy channels for both the intended receiver and an eavesdropper. Title: Design of Short Blocklength Wiretap Channel Codes: Deep Learning and Cryptography Working Hand in Hand
Authors: Vidhi Rana, Rémi A. Chou
Abstract: We design short blocklength codes for the Gaussian wiretap channel under information-theoretic security guarantees. Our approach consists in decoupling the reliability and secrecy constraints in our code design. Title: Reliability-Based Decoding of Complex Low-Density Lattice Codes
Authors: Warangrat Wiriya, Brian M. Kurkoski
Abstract: This paper proposes a low-complexity decoding algorithm for complex low-density lattice codes (CLDLC). The key is a method to approximate an infinite complex Gaussian mixture that occurs at the variable node of the belief propagation (BP) decoder. Title: Combined polar-LDPC design for channels with high noise
Authors: Ilya Dumer, Navid Gharavi
Abstract: We combine polar and LDPC codes to address data correction for various low-power applications. We first use long low-rate LDPC codes that have parity checks of a low weight. Decoding performs several iterations of the belief propagation (BP) algorithm that recalculates the information bits only. Title: A new coded communication modulation scheme in very low frequency communication
Authors: Peipei Cao, Kaijie Zhou
Abstract: Very low frequency (VLF) radio waves have very effective long-distance propagation characteristics. Therefore, they have a wide application prospect in underwater and underground communication. Title: A Low-Complexity EMS Algorithm with Dynamic Message Truncation for Non-Binary LDPC Codes
Authors: Fulong Wang, Ming Zhan, Qian Zhang, Hao Tang, Yunkai Feng, Mingjuan Qiu, Liangxi Liu
Abstract: The extended min-sum (EMS) algorithm is adopted for non-binary low density parity check (NB-LDPC) codes. The high complexity of EMS algorithm is a hot topic in recent years. Aiming at the problem, a low-complexity EMS algorithm based on NB-LDPC codes is proposed. Title: Design of Interleaver under Unbalanced Channels in LDPC-BICM MIMO System
Authors: Rui Zhao, Jingwen Zhang, Xinyi Wang, Dechao Chen
Abstract: The unbalanced channel gain caused by multiple antennas in the multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) wireless system will cause multi-stream data to pass through channels with an unbalanced signal-to-noise ratio (SNR). Title: New McEliece Cryptosystem Based on Polar-LDPC Concatenated Codes as a Post-quantum Cryptography
Authors: Ruyi Wang, Yong Wang, Hao Xie
Abstract: With the increase of computing power of quantum computers, classical cryptography schemes such as RSA and ECC are no longer secure in the era of quantum computers. The Cryptosystem based on coding has the advantage of resisting quantum computing and has a good application prospect in the future. Title: A Random Construction Method of SC-LDPC Code with Changed Check Structure
Authors: Cong Xie, Jian Yang, Hai Tian, Danfeng Zhao, Tongzhou Han
Abstract: In order to meet the reliability requirements of 6G mobile communication, this paper proposes a construction method of spatially coupled low density parity check codes based on PEG algorithm. Authors: Tuofeng Lei, Shuyan Ni, Naipinz Cheng, Xin Song, Aidi Zhang, LingFeng Cheng
Abstract: Sparse code multiple access (SCMA) and low density parity check (LDPC) are two promising technologies in 5G New Radio communications. Title: Design of Nested QC-LDPC Codes for Compute-Forward Multiple Access
Authors: Shenzuo He, Yifei Zhang, Ying Li
Abstract: Compute-forward multiple access (CFMA) is a new-type multiple-access technique in recent years, whose most significant advantage is that it can achieve the whole capacity region of the multiple-access channel (MAC) without time sharing or rate splitting. Title: Index Modulation of OAM-UCA with LDPC Transmission
Authors: Fusheng Zhu, Jin Jiang, Yongjun Li, Chenhong Zhou, Lulu Tang, Zhengrong Lai
Abstract: The Orbital Angular Momentum (OAM) is utilized as a new dimension to improve wireless communication spectral efficiency. combination of OAM and Multi-Input Multi-Output (MIMO) with Uniform Circular Array (UCA) scheme is expected to be easily applied in the existing communication network. Title: A Flexible FPGA Based QC-LDPC Decoder for the Adaptive Coding and Modulation Scheme of Broadband Satellite Communication System
Authors: Xiaxia Cui, Dongdong Wang, Chenhua Sun, Haowei Sun, Ming Zhang
Abstract: We designed a FPGA based LDPC Decoder for decoding Type-I QC-LDPC codes with flexible code rate and code length, by which the channel adaptation could be realized with very fine granularity. Authors: Mingyu Cai, Shufeng Li, Zhicheng Liu
Abstract: Polar codes can approach the Shannon limit with arbitrary binary-input discrete memoryless channels. As one of the most advanced soft-output algorithms, soft cancellation decoding (SCAN) has a better decoding performance than the hard-output algorithm, such as conventional successive-cancellation (SC) decoding. Title: A Bit Flipping Viterbi Decoding Algorithm Based on CRC Check for Convolutional Code
Authors: Yaping Sun, Gaoqi Dou, Hang Yin
Abstract: Soft output Viterbi decoding (SOVA) algorithm, as maximum likelihood decoding, is a convolutional code decoding algorithm commonly used in communication systems. In order to further improve the decoding performance and reduce the bit error rate, a bit flipping Viterbi decoding algorithm based on cyclic redundancy check (CRC) is proposed in this paper. Title: An Efficient Two-Dimension OTFS-NOMA Scheme Based on Heterogenous Mobility Users Grouping
Authors: Ziqi Kang, Hang Zhao, Hua Wang
Abstract: Orthogonal time frequency space (OTFS) modulation recently has been widely studied for its superior performance in high-mobility scenarios. In order to explore the efficiency potential and performance, the concept of non-orthogonal multiple access (NOMA) is extended to OTFS as a key technique to enhance its spectral efficiency. Title: Adaptive Modulation and Coding in Satellite-Integrated 5G Communication System
Authors: Dechao Chen, Jingwen Zhang, Rui Zhao
Abstract: In order to achieve wide coverage, high-speed communication and the global interconnection of everything, satellites communications and 5G mobile communication will be complementary and integrated in the future. Title: A Turbo Coded LoRa-Index Modulation Scheme for IoT Communication
Authors: Shixiang An, Zhiping Lu, Hua Wang, Quantao Yu
Abstract: Long-Range (LoRa) is one of the emerging technologies in low-power wide-area networks (LPWANs). Title: Research on LDPC channel coding technology in satellite communication system
Authors: Shikang Nie, Guangting Li, Xin Liu, Xin Zhang, Chenchen Lin, Yifeng He
Abstract: This paper discusses in detail the development of several research hotspots of Low-Density Parity Check (LDPC) codes and their joint optimization design with other communication technologies, and points out that LDPC codes have broad development and application space in the future communication field.

Abstract: This paper assesses the 5G New Radio (NR) physical layer for satellite downlink transmission requiring power amplification (PA) with low backoff, thereby suffering significant nonlinear distortion. Authors: Longyu Ma, Chiu Wing Sham, Chun Yan Lo, Xinchao Zhong
Abstract: Extracting and analyzing iris textures for biometric recognition has been extensively studied. Authors: Xiangming Cai, Luyao Hu, Weikai Xu, Lin Wang
Abstract: Given the high transmission loss, severe Doppler spread and large multipath delay in the underwater acoustic (UWA) channel, the channel estimation and tracking in the conventional orthogonal frequency division multiplexing (OFDM) based UWA systems are severely unaffordable. Authors: Aravind Aji, Kurunandan Jain, Prabhakar Krishnan
Abstract: The exponential development in Quantum Computing is becoming a threat to the existing asymmetric cryptographic algorithms like RSA (Rivest, Shamir, Adleman).
